title: Research: Agent Collaboration & Communication Layer

Compare with OpenClaw Lane Queue model vs Strands Swarm/Graph. Research agent-to-agent notification, task handoff, shared memory, and collaborative problem-solving. Output: docs/research/collaboration/

title: Improve lead agent workflow: merge-up, research-first, document-as-you-go
description:
Update .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md with three workflow improvements:

1. MERGE-UP CONSOLIDATION: After all builders complete and pass review, the lead must merge each builder branch into its own lead branch (git merge in worktree), then send ONE merge_ready to coordinator for the lead branch. This eliminates per-builder merges at coordinator level.

2. RESEARCH-FIRST WORKFLOW: Before implementing or documenting, leads and builders should investigate, deep-dive, analyze the problem space. Read existing code/docs, use MCP research tools, understand the landscape BEFORE writing. The scout phase exists but needs to be emphasized as mandatory for complex tasks — explore first, architect second, implement third.

3. DOCUMENT-AS-YOU-GO: As implementation proceeds, builders should document their work inline — comments where non-obvious, README updates, architecture notes. This keeps things transparent and makes it easy for other agents to pick up the work. No separate documentation phase — weave it into implementation.

File to modify: .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md
Also review: .overstory/agent-defs/builder.md, scout.md for consistency
closeReason: Merged workflow improvements to lead.md, builder.md, scout.md: merge-up consolidation, research-first, document-as-you-go, periodic rebase

title: Update lead agent hooks to allow git merge/rebase for merge-up consolidation
description: The new merge-up consolidation workflow requires leads to git merge builder branches into their lead branch and periodically git rebase on main. Current PreToolUse hooks block git merge, git rebase for lead agents. Need to update hooks to allow these operations for leads.

title: Research: Skill format compatibility — OpenClaw, Claude Code, AgentCore Gateway
description:
Deep research into skill formats across platforms and how Chimera can achieve compatibility:

1. OpenClaw SKILL.md format (v1 and v2) — what does it look like, how are skills loaded/executed?
2. Claude Code skills format — how are Claude Code skills structured (frontmatter, content, triggers)?
3. MCP tools as skills — MCP servers expose tools, can these be wrapped as Chimera skills?
4. AgentCore Gateway — does it support serving/routing skills? Can it act as a skill registry?
5. Strands tools — how does Strands define and load tools? @tool decorator pattern?
6. Compatibility layer — can Chimera natively support OpenClaw SKILL.md AND Claude Code skills AND MCP tools?
7. Skill marketplace architecture — ClawHub concept, discovery, versioning, trust tiers

Output: docs/research/skills/
closeReason: Merged skill compatibility research: 4 docs (~6600 lines) covering OpenClaw/Claude Code/MCP/Strands formats, universal adapter design, marketplace architecture

Should work WITH the user (collaborative) and autonomously (self-improving). Output: docs/research/aws-account-agent/

title: Research: Infrastructure-as-capability — agents that build and operate AWS resources

Builder branches ready for merge.

title: Research: Autonomous problem-solving swarm — vague tasks to complete solutions
description: Research how agent swarms handle vague/ambiguous requests: (1) Task decomposition — break vague ask into concrete subtasks, (2) Blocker detection and resolution — agents identify what's missing and fix it, (3) Multi-agent collaboration — planner, researcher, builder, validator roles, (4) Human-in-the-loop — when to ask for clarification vs proceed autonomously, (5) Progressive refinement — start with POC, iterate toward production. Example: 'set up monitoring for our microservices' → discover services → evaluate CloudWatch vs Grafana → deploy dashboards → configure alarms → document. Output: docs/research/aws-account-agent/
closeReason: Research documentation completed: Created Progressive Refinement document (1,659 lines) covering 5-stage lifecycle from POC to production, and research index (439 lines) cataloging the autonomous problem-solving series. Total 2,098 lines documenting feedback loops, quality gates, blocker resolution, and human-in-the-loop patterns. Committed as be219b8, mulch learnings recorded.

title: Research: Structured activity logging — agent decisions documented, not buried in CloudTrail
description: Research how Chimera documents everything it does in structured format: (1) Decision logs — why the agent chose approach A over B, with Well-Architected justification, (2) Action audit trail — every API call, resource created, config changed, stored in DDB/S3 not just CloudTrail, (3) Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) auto-generated for infrastructure changes, (4) Runbook auto-generation — agent documents what it built and how to operate it, (5) Status dashboards — real-time view of what agents are doing, what they built, what changed. No post-mortem digging through CloudWatch needed. Output: docs/research/aws-account-agent/
closeReason: Completed 7-document research series on structured activity logging (182KB total). Created: 00-Index (overview), 01-Architecture (system design, storage, queries), 02-Decision-Logs (reasoning capture with Well-Architected), 03-Action-Audit-Trail (beyond CloudTrail), 04-Auto-Generated-ADRs (architecture decision records), 05-Runbook-Auto-Generation (operational docs), 06-Real-Time-Dashboards (live visibility via EventBridge/WebSocket). All docs follow research pattern with frontmatter, TOC, executive summary, technical deep-dives, code examples, key takeaways.

title: Research: Deep AWS SDK integration — boto3/SDK/CLI as agent capabilities
description: Research how Chimera agents deeply integrate with AWS: (1) Which AWS APIs should be first-class tools (EC2, S3, Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, EventBridge, CloudWatch, IAM, CloudFormation, ECS, RDS, etc.), (2) boto3 for Python Lambda tools, AWS SDK v3 for TypeScript, (3) MCP servers for AWS services (existing + custom), (4) Cross-service orchestration (Step Functions), (5) IAM scoping — least-privilege per agent/tenant, (6) Multi-region operations. Every AWS service is accessible via API — the agent should be able to interact with any of them to complete tasks. Output: docs/research/aws-account-agent/
closeReason: Research complete: 6 docs + index (6,881 lines) in docs/research/aws-account-agent/. Covers AWS API tools, SDK patterns (boto3/v3), MCP servers, Step Functions orchestration, IAM scoping (role chains, boundaries, Cedar integration), and multi-region operations. 3 builders completed, all committed and verified.

title: Research: Continuous self-improvement — agents that find and fix their own bottlenecks
description: Research how Chimera continuously improves itself: (1) Performance profiling — agents measure their own latency, cost, accuracy, (2) Bottleneck detection — identify slow tools, expensive operations, failed tasks, (3) Auto-optimization — generate improved prompts, switch models, add caching, scale resources, (4) Capability expansion — detect missing skills and build them, (5) Cost optimization — find idle resources, rightsize, spot instances, (6) Feedback loops — learn from user corrections. Reference: packages/core/src/evolution/ (model-router, prompt-optimizer, experiment-runner). Output: docs/research/aws-account-agent/
